to: Linux Codex / Guided AI Journey Website and Tools
summary: Guided AI Journey sends a minimal system signal, M365 creates a CRM - New Signals item, and M365 sends a signed receipt acknowledgement back to the fixed Journey endpoint. The live loop was proved with synthetic portalEventId db8d3f91-002b-4729-b6ac-556ee5813d3d, CRM item #25, successful M365 callback, successful Teams alert flow, and Journey ledger status crm_received. Follow-on source-display proof added Lead source detail to CRM provenance and the Teams alert.

adamApproval.scope: M365-to-Guided-AI-Journey CRM receipt callback for the B7 portalEventId handshake

m365CurrentState: CRM - New Signals is the source of truth.
Existing Journey Microsoft Forms proof created CRM item #21 and a Teams alert.
The custom HTTP intake receiver preserves Journey portalEventId/correlation metadata in SourceText.
The live custom HTTP intake receiver includes a signed M365-to-Journey acknowledgement action after Create_item.
B7 live proof created CRM item #25, sent a successful callback to Journey, and Journey read back crm_received.
Lead-source display proof created CRM item #27 with Lead source detail = Journey admin invite, and the Teams alert flow posted successfully.
notApprovedByThisPacket: No external email or client message from M365.
No guest invite, permission change, CRM merge, or Planner/calendar task.
No callback to a Journey-provided arbitrary URL.
No tenant secret, acknowledgement secret, .env value, bearer token, or private key committed to git or DirectLink.

receiveContract.receiver: Existing custom HTTP intake endpoint from WINDOWS_TO_JOURNEY__custom-intake-form-spec.json. Endpoint URL and x-intake-secret remain server-side only.

headers.x-intake-secret: server-side secret already supplied out of band

minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.correlationId: same as portalEventId unless Journey needs a separate send-attempt id
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.journeyLeadId: Journey dashboard lead/signal id, if available
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.leadSourceDetail: optional friendly label; M365 derives one from sourceAction if absent
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.portalDeepLink: Journey operator/admin deep link, if safe
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.leadContext: short system-generated context; do not ask the client for this
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.needSummary: optional; if absent M365 derives a basic invite-signal summary
minimumPayloadForInviteSignal.situation: optional IntentPath choice if Journey already knows it

crmMapping.Title: Guided AI Journey - best available name/org/email
crmMapping.NeedSummary: needSummary, else leadContext, else generated invite-signal summary
crmMapping.SourceText: human-readable fields plus Lead source detail, Journey signal metadata, portal event id, correlation id, company/engagement/invite ids, organization id, lead id, role, source action, portal deep link, and provenance

deploymentGateBeforeM365LiveCallback: crm_lifecycle_events migration exists in production Supabase.
CRM_LIFECYCLE_ACK_SECRET or equivalent server-side env is configured in Vercel production.
Linux-side synthetic send has created a real portalEventId.
The real ack secret is stored locally under .local/flow-builder.
The live M365 HTTP intake receiver is verified or updated.

m365ReceiverState.displayName: GAIL - Custom site intake to CRM (create-only, HTTP)

m365WillNotUse: A callback URL supplied inside the incoming lead payload

plannedM365AckPayload.companyId: same companyId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.engagementId: same engagementId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.inviteId: same inviteId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.journeyInviteId: same journeyInviteId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.journeyOrganizationId: same journeyOrganizationId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.journeyLeadId: same journeyLeadId/dashboardLeadId received from Journey, if any
plannedM365AckPayload.processedAt: ISO-8601 timestamp from M365 acknowledgement flow
plannedM365AckPayload.ackGeneratedAt: ISO-8601 timestamp from M365 acknowledgement flow

noRealSubjectTest.recommended: Use an internal synthetic admin/invite event from Journey, not a real client.

payloadOverrides.leadContext: Internal B7 proof that Journey invite signal reaches CRM and receives an acknowledgement.

expectedM365Result: One CRM - New Signals item with IntakeSource = Guided AI Journey.
SourceText contains Portal event id, Correlation id, and Journey invite id.
One internal Teams New Signal post.
M365 acknowledgement callback is sent only after CRM item exists, once the Journey ack endpoint is available.

m365AnswersToJourneyQuestions.portalEventIdStorage: Current builder preserves portalEventId in SourceText. A first-class SharePoint column is recommended later for strict idempotency/dedupe.
m365AnswersToJourneyQuestions.signedAckAfterCreate: Supported locally by create-http-intake-flow.js when fixed endpoint and real secret files exist under .local/flow-builder. Adam approved the live callback build on 2026-06-25; remaining gates are real secret, Journey production readiness, receiver update, and proof.
m365AnswersToJourneyQuestions.targetListForV1: Continue landing portal lifecycle events in CRM - New Signals for v1 so the proven alert/triage lane remains intact.

clientFrictionGuidance.systemInviteSignal: Zero client-facing questions. Send what Journey already knows from the invite/admin action.
clientCompletionForm: Prefer prefilled name, email, and organization.
Ask only one free-text prompt: What would make this useful?
Ask one intent/path choice only if it materially helps routing.
Include one consent checkbox when a human is submitting.
Do not ask the client for IDs or metadata Journey already has.

nextProof: B7 live proof complete; next work is refinement only: optional first-class portalEventId column, Journey retry/replay operator action, and continued secret hygiene.
